blockers.title: TypeScript does not compile
blockers.evidence: `./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 2: `convex/members.ts(913,7)` returns `pipeline_locked` but the annotated `getMySettings` return type omits it; `src/portal/Settings.tsx(76,18)` and `tests/convex/toggleSafeguards.test.ts(73,22),(85,22)` then fail on that missing property.
blockers.required_fix: Add `pipeline_locked: boolean` to the `getMySettings` return type or remove the field and UI dependency, then rerun typecheck, build, and tests.

blockers.title: Pipeline consent still bypasses the required opt-in review through join and claim
blockers.evidence: Spec says pipeline consent has exactly one path through `setPipelineOptIn` and no consent row may skip attestation + review (`specs/optin-toggles.spec.md:49-50`). But `JoinApp` submits `pipeline: values.pipeline` (`src/portal/JoinApp.tsx:199-203`), `ClaimFlow` submits `pipeline: showPipeline ? pipeline : false` (`src/portal/ClaimFlow.tsx:95-99`), and the server writes true pipeline consent rows in those paths (`convex/members.ts:469-483`, `convex/members.ts:829-843`) without opening `pipelineEligibilityReviews` or setting `pipeline_state = review_pending`. The stale test at `tests/convex/joinSafeguards.test.ts:117-129` explicitly expects this non-compliant behavior.
blockers.required_fix: Remove the pipeline checkbox from join/claim or force pipeline consent to false there. The only way to write a true pipeline consent row should be `setPipelineOptIn` after login, with attestation and review creation. Update tests to assert that invariant.

blockers.title: Server-side toggle authorization and revocation are incomplete
blockers.evidence: `setDirectoryVisible` and `setPipelineOptIn` do not require `lifecycle_state === "active"` before enabling visibility or opening a review (`convex/members.ts:920-1001`), unlike existing profile/consent mutations. Also, `setPipelineOptIn` refuses all non-standard lanes before checking `args.value` (`convex/members.ts:972-984`), while `writeConsent` refuses all pipeline writes (`convex/members.ts:536-545`), so a reclassified member cannot revoke a stale true pipeline consent. The vault requires pipeline consent to be revocable (`02 Privacy & Data Protection (Decision).md:55,66`).
blockers.required_fix: Gate enabling actions on active lifecycle and eligible lane. Still allow disabling/revoking visibility and pipeline consent for the owning authenticated member even when the lane is now ineligible, closing pending reviews and writing an explicit false consent row.

non_blocking_notes: `npm run check` and `npm run test` could not run in this read-only sandbox because Astro/Vite tried to write generated/cache files; `tsc --noEmit` was used and found real compile errors.
No secrets were found in the reviewed diff. Added member-facing Settings/Dashboard strings did not contain em dashes.

spec_compliance.criterion: 1. Schema adds directory_visible, pipeline_state, and pipelineEligibilityReviews indexes
2. setDirectoryVisible keyed to auth user, refuses minor/restricted_unknown, audited
spec_compliance.evidence: Auth/lane/audit are present in `convex/members.ts:920-950`, but enabling lacks an active-lifecycle server check.

spec_compliance.criterion: 3. setPipelineOptIn is the sole attested true-consent path and handles OFF safely
spec_compliance.evidence: `setPipelineOptIn` itself opens reviews, but join/claim can still write true pipeline consent without review, and ineligible/reclassified members cannot revoke.

spec_compliance.criterion: 4. decidePipelineReview fallback approves/rejects pending reviews and audits reviewer
5. Settings UI exposes both toggles with locked minor state
spec_compliance.evidence: UI intent exists in `src/portal/Settings.tsx`, but the TypeScript errors make this unshippable.
Server strips mentor options in `convex/members.ts:220-229`; UI hides them via `Dashboard.tsx:25-28,136-139` and `ProfileEditor.tsx:390-399`.
No partner search/query surface was added in the diff.

spec_compliance.criterion: 8. Tests cover pure rules and suites stay green
spec_compliance.evidence: Typecheck fails, tests cannot be considered green, and an existing test asserts the now-forbidden join-time true pipeline consent path.
Join and claim still expose/write pipeline-searchable consent outside the new settings toggle path.

summary: DO-NOT-SHIP: the branch does not compile, and the core safety invariant for pipeline consent is not actually enforced.
